title: Laundry Facilities - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Laundry Facilities 53 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Homeless Families Homeless Women Homeless Youth ASTORIA RESCUE MISSION COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves three community meals including breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Operates a clothing closet, showers, and a laundry facility allowing one load per week. Accepts donations of hygiene products such as shampoo, conditioner, body wash, toilet paper, and toothpaste. 62 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Breakfast: 7 days per week 7am Lunch: 7 days per week noon Dinner: 7 days per week 5:30pm Showers: 7 days per week 3:30pm-5pm Laundry: 7 days per week 3:30pm-5pm Clothing Closet: 7 days per week 11am-5pm Email (503) 325-6243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLUB HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a recovery drop-in center with support services for substance addiction, including support groups and an employment center. Additional services include laundry facilities, showers, and snacks. Services are available for individuals at any stage of recovery. 100 SE Cleveland Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Drop-In Center: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Showers and laundry: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm (503) 933-2440 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT TEEN DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Offers a food pantry, help applying for state benefits such as OHP and SNAP, showers, laundry facilities. Also offers assistance with educational needs such as re-enrollment, school supplies, and specialized information and referral. Eligibility: Ages 11-24 that are homeless and unaccompanied by an adult Hours: Tuesday/Thursday noon-4pm Email (800) 660-0934 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC CHARITIES HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides drop-in services including information and referral, showers, laundry facilities, a community meal, snacks, and coffee. Offers access to a phone, community voicemail, mailing address, and computers. 2740 SE Powell Boulevard #5 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Non-parenting women age 25 and older who are experiencing homelessness. Well-mannered pets are allowed. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Lunch: 11:30am-1pm Email (971) 222-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES SPRINGFIELD OASIS DROP-IN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A collaborative project that provides homeless families priority access to a coordinated set of services offered at multiple locations in the G Street neighborhood. The participating organizations function as a campus, with all services within easy walking distance. Services include: -A safe place to be during the day -Access to laundry, showers, and restroom facilities -A place to prepare and eat simple meals -Help finding affordable housing and employment -Access to additional school and community based resources 1025 G Street Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness and at least one child age 17 and younger in their care Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-4pm, Wednesday 8am-6pm, closed holidays Email (541) 345-3628 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE LAUNDRY AND SHOWER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shower and laundry services. Individuals may do 2 loads of laundry per week. Dryer access is limited to 30 minutes per load. Services are available on a first-come, first-serve basis. 231 Lyon Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 791-3411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHURCH @ THE PARK SAFE PARKING C@P SERVICE CENTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a place for people to take showers, do laundry, access case management services, and be indoors. 2640 Portland Road NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 Hours: Monday-Friday noon-5pm Email (971) 375-7491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND THE WASH HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry services. Users can drop off their items to be laundered and pick up the same day or the next day. Customers will be given a ticket with their pick up date and time and are allowed to bring one bag per day with a max weight limit of 40 pounds. The Wash House is funded by the City of Portland's Impact Reduction Program (IRP) and staffed by participants of Ground Score Association's GLITTER program. 2609 SE 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 9am-4pm; except holidays Email 311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS SERVICE CENTER SHOWERS AND LAUNDRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry facilities and hot showers with shampoo, soap, and a towel. Change of underclothes provided when available. Showers limited to 15 minutes. 8800 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Showers: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-1pm Laundry: Tuesday-Thursday 1pm-4pm Email (503) 771-7914 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM ASSISTANCE FOR PEOPLE WITHOUT HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry, showers, propane, tarps, and blankets. Also provides sleeping bags when available. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as homeless Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONNECTED LANE COUNTY SPARK AT BOOTH KELLY DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a youth drop-in center. Services include job finding assistance, resume and cover letter support, interview practice, career mentorship, assistance with completing college and scholarship applications, information and referral to housing and healthcare community resources, and access to shower and laundry facilities. 303 S 5th Street Suite 150 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 14-24 Hours: Wednesday-Thursday 3pm-6pm, Friday varies by month Email (541) 654-4095 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORE CORE LAUNDRY SERVICES LAUNDRY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to laundry facilities. 247 N Fir Street Sisters, OR 97759 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday 1pm-3pm Get Directions More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CI HYGIENE HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides showers, laundry services, clothing, and hygiene products. 14625 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Friday 10:30am-3pm, Tuesday-Thursday 9:30am-2pm Email (503) 477-9742 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DESCHUTES COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH THE DROP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe and supportive space for youth and young adults. Services include computers and wifi, charging stations for devices, games, music, movies, laundry, information on social services, coffee and snacks, and a quiet corner with activities. 1340 NW Wall Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 14-29 Hours: Monday/Wednesday 2pm-5:30pm, Tuesday 5pm-6:30pm, Friday 1pm-4:30pm (541) 797-3623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV DAY CENTER HIV SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center serving hot breakfast and lunches, access to phones and computers, and recreational activities such as art therapy, games, and video-based media. Also provides clothing, showers, mail services, and hygiene supplies. Guests have access to laundry services, massages, acupuncture, foot care, and haircuts on a sign-up basis. 2941 NE Ainsworth Street Basement Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Individuals who are HIV-positive and have incomes no greater than 300% of the federal poverty level Hours: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday 1pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 460-3822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, clothing, laundry, personal hygiene items, showers, job training classes, mentoring programs, referrals to other resources, and access to telephone and computer services. For the winter season, offering tents, sleeping bags, and blankets. 834 Monroe Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 with a focus on individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 11am-6:30pm, Friday 11am-3pm Lunch: noon-2pm Dinner: 5pm Email (541) 344-5583 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER STREET LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term overnight shelter, two meals per day, and cleanup facilities for 30 homeless and street dependent youth attempting to make changes in their lives. Youth may access clothing, medical assistance, and employment education support. When severe weather is declared by the city, 5 additional beds will open. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Displaced youth ages 16-24. Youth must be working with a case manager on a plan in order to access a bed on a continuing basis. Hours: 7 days per week 9pm-9am Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK MADRAS SHELTER SERVICE CENTER MADRAS SHELTER DAYTIME SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center, meals, case management, showers, and laundry services. Volunteer opportunities available. 61 NW Oak Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Unrestricted with a focus on individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Drop in center: 7 days per week 7am-1pm and 6pm-10pm Lunch: 7 days per week 11am-12:30pm Dinner: 7 days per week 6pm-8pm Showers: Monday/Friday 7:30am-12:30pm Laundry: Thursdays 7am-1pm drop off, Friday pick up times vary Email (458) 800-4208 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER JUST COMPASSION UNITY LAUNDROMAT LAUNDRY SERVICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry services with detergent and quarters provided. A sack lunch and beverage is available. 11945 SW Pacific Highway Suite 123 Portland, OR 97223 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 2pm-4pm Email (503) 624-4666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LAUNDRY LOVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers laundry services. Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Varies by location (805) 665-3078 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEBOAT SERVICES DAYTIME NAVIGATION SHELTER LIFEBOAT DAY SHELTER AND NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center with access to socks, laundry facilities, Wi-Fi, computer and telephone access, fax, coffee, snacks, a clothing closet, assistance in navigating social services, and specialized information and referral. Camping gear may be available, depending on donations. 1076 Franklin Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 6am-6pm, Thursday/Friday 8am-6pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS NEW ROADS DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop-in center with laundry facilities, phone use, temporary mailing address, showers, and daily meals. Also provides street outreach, case management, mental health counseling, education, and assistance finding housing. Eligible youth will be added to a central housing referral list for all programs they qualify for. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Homeless and at-risk youth ages 16-24 Hours: -Drop-in Center: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm -Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am-9:30am -Lunch: 7 days per week noon-1pm -Snack: 7 days per week 3pm-3:30pm -Dinner: 7 days per week 4pm-5pm Email (541) 686-4310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVEONE COMMUNITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY BRAX LAUNDRY ESTACADA LOVEONE LAUNDRY EVENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry services including detergent and softeners, if needed. Participants are limited to a maximum of 3 loads of laundry. A hotmeal and personal grooming supplies may be available, dependent upon available funding. All services available on a first-come, first-serve basis. 990 NW Wade Street Estacada, OR 97023 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month 11am-1pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVEONE COMMUNITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY BRAX LAUNDRY MOLALLA LOVEONE LAUNDRY EVENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry services including detergent and softeners, if needed. Participants are limited to a maximum of 3 loads of laundry. A hotmeal and personal grooming supplies may be available, dependent upon available funding. All services available on a first-come, first-serve basis. 10515 SE 42nd Avenue Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 2nd Monday of the month. 6pm-8pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVEONE COMMUNITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY QED LAUNDRY LOVEONE LAUNDRY EVENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry services including detergent and softeners, if needed. All services available on a first-come, first-serve basis. 18020 SE McLoughlin Boulevard Milwaukie, OR 97267 Eligibility: 1st and 3rd Wednesday of the month 6pm-8pm Hours: Varies by site Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVEONE COMMUNITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY WELCHES LAUNDROMAT LOVEONE LAUNDRY EVENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry services including detergent and softeners, if needed. All services available on a first-come, first-serve basis. 24525 E Welches Road Welches, OR 97067 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 1st and 3rd Wednesday of the month 11am-5pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARLA'S MALL BULLDOG BUBBLES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides laundry services through a work experience program for high school students. 2500 E Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Laundry: Unrestricted Volunteers: Baker School District high school students Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 7:30am-11:30am and 1pm-4pm, closed holidays. Hours are subject to change. Summer hours: Monday-Wednesday 7:30am-11:30am and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 524-2309 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL THE GLORIA CENTER MONTHLY DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to showers and laundry facilities, hygiene items, a hot meal, warming and cooling supplies (blankets, tarps, hats, gloves, socks, hand and toe warmers, hydration packets, cooling towels, sunscreen, and lip balm), and connections to community resources. 2505 W 7th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: January-October Last Friday of the month 9am-3pm, hot meal noon-3pm November-December 3rd Friday of the month 9am-3pm, hot meal noon-3pm Email (541) 298-5131 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOUNTAIN VIEW COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT SAFE PARKING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides safe parking facilities for community members living out of their car or a RV, and also has shower and laundry facilities. Partners with two local low-income housing complexes to provide case management support for placement into permanent housing. 1475 SW 35th Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Permanent housing: age 55 and older or families with children Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 527-0028 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-barrier day center that offers computers and charging stations, a temporary mailing address, laundry facilities, restrooms, showers, and resources. Access to the day center is in four, 3-hour time blocks. To enter, participants need a ticket, and 25 tickets are distributed for each time block on a first-come, first-served basis. No ticket is required to pick up mail, which is available up to four times per day. Do Good Multnomah runs shelter and bridge housing programs on site to help people continue on their path to permanent housing. Referrals come through the Day Center. 333 SW Park Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Age 18 and older, pets allowed Hours: Day Center: 7 days per week 8am-8pm (intake stops at 7:45pm), closed the 4th Friday of the month from noon-3pm Mail pick up: 9am-10am, noon-1pm, 3pm-4pm, 6pm-7pm Time Block 1: 8am-10:45am Time Block 2: 11am-1:45pm Time Block 3: 2pm-4:45pm Time Block 4: 5pm-7:45pm Email (503) 988-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DOWNTOWN DROP-IN DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 820 SW Oak Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH CONNECT DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 43 SE 102 Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 12-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. Also connects youth to other services like education, job training, employment, housing, LGBTQIA2S+ supports, and more. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH & ADVOCACY PROJECT HOMELESS OUTREACH AND ADVOCACY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Services include information and referral, laundry, clothing, showers, lunch, mailbox access, mental health support groups, and peer support services. Also connects clients with medical services at West Salem Medical Clinic. 694 Church Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Individuals who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 2pm-4pm Email (503) 588-5827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHRA OHRA COMMUNITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing services, case management, information and referral, mailing address, telephone, benefits and services assistance, job search-related printing, resume creation, hygiene supplies, and education opportunities. Showers and laundry are available twice a week. Each individual who showers can do a small load of laundry. 2350 Ashland Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers and Laundry: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm. First come, first serve. Email (541) 631-2235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A drop in center that provides basic needs services such as meals, recreation/activities, showers, laundry, phone, mailing address, and computers. Also offers a safe space for LGBTQ youth. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are experiencing homelessness and are eligible to enter the Homeless Youth Continuum Hours: Drop-In: Tuesday/Thursday/Sunday 1pm-6pm Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10am Lunch: Sunday-Friday 1pm-2pm Dinner: Sunday-Friday 5pm-6pm Email (503) 535-3800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, showers, laundry, temporary mailing addresses, clothing provisions, cell phone charging, TriMet tickets, shelter and housing referrals, and connections to various community partners and services. 1440 SE 21st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on individuals who are homeless Hours: Center: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-5pm Meals: Breakfast 9am, sack lunches noon, hot meal 3pm Email (971) 713-3930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT TRACK RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center offering lunch, food boxes, laundry, showers, computers with internet access, assistance with housing applications and cover letters, and temporary mailing addresses. May also provide pet food, hygiene supplies, and grab and go foods as supplies allow. 501 3rd Street La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday 9am-3pm, Friday 10am-2pm (541) 962-7994 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROLLING HILLS COMMUNITY CHURCH WASH & WORSHIP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a hot breakfast, showers, and access to laundry services. 3550 SW Borland Road Tualatin, OR 97062 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Sunday 8:30am-10:30am Email (503) 683-5900 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSE HAVEN ROSE HAVEN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that assists with food, showers, laundry facilities, clothing, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, and temporary mailing addresses. Advocates are available to help connect individuals to resources and provide emotional support. Outdoor gear provided when available. 1740 NW Glisan Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Women, transgender, non-binary, and gender-expansive adults and their minor children Hours: -Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-noon for shelter, services from 8:30am-11:30am -Tuesday/Thursday 8am-3:30pm for shelter, services from 8:30am-11:30am and 1pm-3:30pm -Wednesday 1pm-3:30pm for guests with minor children only Email (503) 248-6364 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SET FREE CHRISTIAN FELLOWSHIP GOD'S FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers services for the unhoused and low-income individuals and families including a food pantry, clothing distribution, and hot meals with bottled water. Shower and laundry services also available for those who are unhoused. 1032 W Main Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unhoused and low-income individuals and families. Shower and laundry services for unhoused people. Hours: Hot meals: Monday/Wednesday 9:30am, Friday noon (first-come, first served) Showers/Laundry: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-noon Clothing: Friday 9:30am-12:30pm Food pantry: Friday 9:30am-1:30pm Email (541) 773-4004 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL MEDFORD URBAN REST STOP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides showers and laundry facilities. 2424 N Pacific Highway Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Households experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 9am-1pm Email (541) 772-3828 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY EUGENE SERVICE STATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center. Services include laundry facilities, showers, meals, phone access, voicemail services, clothing, blankets, personal care supplies, job search assistance, and information and referral to other services. Visitors can use indoor, climate-controlled seating and outdoor, shaded picnic tables. 450 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Low-income and homeless adults without minor children Hours: 7 days per week 8:30am-5pm Breakfast: 7 days per week 8:30am Lunch: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday noon, Wednesday 11am Showers and laundry: 7 days per week 8:30am-noon and 2pm-5pm Email (541) 689-6747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center with laundry, showers, internet access, mail services, clothing vouchers, on-site clothing, and hygiene supplies. Assistance in obtaining identification documents. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 and pregnant women (proof of pregnancy required) who are experiencing homelessness Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL THE DALLES SHOWERS AND LAUNDRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers laundry and shower facilities. 315 W 3rd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 11am-4pm Email (541) 296-9566 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in accessing services and agencies to help people transition out of homelessness into stable housing. The center offers showers, laundry, haircuts, clothing, lockers, and mailing address service. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays: 8:30am-3pm. All service intakes end 15 minutes before close. 1st Thursday and 3rd Tuesday of the month the Resource Center closes at noon. Accepts donations of paper products, towels, snacks, bottled water, juice, personal care supplies, socks, underwear, and seasonally appropriate clothing. 211 SE Chapman Place Corvallis, OR 97333
